trigger_keywords: test cases for feature3 2 class options

context_pointers.vague_reference: apply the same error classification used in the rate limiting module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The error message must be exactly 'rate_limit_exceeded' matching the error code definition for HTTP 403 in the rate limiting feature.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_1_offset_to_line.json.description: Maps absolute character offsets within a text buffer to their zero-based line numbers. The input is a multi-line source string and a list of character offsets; each offset is resolved to the line that contains it. Offsets at or beyond the final newline resolve to the last line, and an empty buffer resolves every offset to line zero.

cases.expected_output:
offset=0 line=0
offset=3 line=0
offset=4 line=1
offset=7 line=1
offset=8 line=2
offset=13 line=2

feature1_2_line_to_offset.json.description: Maps zero-based line numbers to the absolute character offset where each line begins. The input is a multi-line source string and a list of line numbers; line numbers beyond the last line clamp to the start offset of the last line, and an empty buffer maps every line to offset zero.

cases.expected_output:
line=0 offset=0
line=1 offset=4
line=2 offset=8
line=3 offset=8

feature2_1_delimited_files.json.description: Parses a snippet that declares several named virtual files using begin/end markers of the form '{$ begin <name> $}' ... '{$ end <name> $}'. The output is the set of file names and their contents in declaration order; each captured file body is terminated with a trailing newline.

feature2_2_plain_snippet.json.description: Parses a snippet that contains no begin/end markers. The whole input is treated as a single default file named main.dart whose content is the input with surrounding whitespace trimmed.

feature2_3_invalid_markers.json.description: Rejects a snippet whose begin/end markers are unbalanced (for example a new begin marker appearing before the previous file was closed). The parse fails with a normalized error category identifying the kind of structural problem.

feature3_1_class_validity.json.description: Decides whether a marker token activates the embeddable playground. A token is valid when it contains the activation keyword (optionally with a leading prefix); a token referencing a different, unrelated keyword is rejected.

feature3_2_class_options.json.description: Extracts colon-delimited key-value options from a valid marker token. Each option is written as 'key-value'; the output lists every parsed option (keys sorted) together with the validity flag. Values may include characters such as percent signs and units.

cases.expected_output:
valid=true
ga_id=example1
height=400px
mode=html
run=true
split=50
theme=dark
width=100%

feature4_extract_html_body.json.description: Extracts the inner content of the body region from an HTML document. Well-formed documents yield the trimmed body content; documents without a body region yield empty content; input that is not a full HTML document is returned unchanged; malformed closing tags inside the body are preserved. The output reports the length of the extracted content followed by the content itself.

feature5_1_stream_source.json.description: Binds a value stream to a target so that each value pushed onto the stream is propagated to the target. The output reports the latest value received by the target and how many times the target was updated.

feature5_2_property_source.json.description: Binds one observable property to another so that changes in the source are mirrored on the target. An explicit flush pushes the source's current value to the target immediately. The output reports the target's final value and its update count.

feature5_3_function_target.json.description: Binds an observable property to a callback target so that the property's value is delivered to the callback. An explicit flush invokes the callback with the source's current value. The output reports the value received by the callback and how many times it was invoked.

feature5_4_cancel.json.description: Cancels an active binding so that further source changes are no longer propagated to the target. Changes emitted before cancellation are delivered; changes emitted after cancellation are ignored. The output reports the target's final value and update count.

feature6_1_retrieve.json.description: A scoped registry that stores a single value per type key. Looking up a type before anything is registered yields nothing; after registering a value it can be retrieved by the same type key. The output reports the lookup result before and after registration.

feature6_2_scoped_zone.json.description: Exposes the active registry only within a dynamically scoped execution region. Outside any region the ambient instance is absent; while running inside the region the registry's instance becomes the ambient one and its registered values are visible; after the region exits the ambient instance is absent again.

feature7_1_load_by_id.json.description: Loads a saved code document by its identifier over an HTTP boundary. A valid identifier returns the document and its primary file content; an unknown identifier and an over-quota response are surfaced as normalized error categories. The output reports the requested URL, the HTTP status observed, and either the primary file content or the error category.

cases.expected_output:
request=https://api.github.com/gists/12345678901234567890123456789012
status=200
main.dart=This is some dart code!

feature7_2_load_by_sample.json.description: Loads a documentation code sample by its sample identifier from the samples endpoint. A valid sample returns a single primary file; an unknown sample is surfaced as a normalized not-found category. The output reports the requested URL, the HTTP status, and either the primary file length or the error category.

feature7_3_load_from_repo.json.description: Loads a multi-file exercise from a source repository by first fetching a metadata descriptor and then fetching each listed file. The descriptor supplies the exercise name and the file list; an optional branch reference is appended to every request. A file may declare an alternate path that is used to fetch its content while keeping its logical name. The output reports the first requested URL, the HTTP status, the exercise name, and the content of a selected file.

cases.expected_output:
request=https://api.github.com/repos/owner/repo/contents/basic/dartpad_metadata.yaml
status=200
description=A Dart Exercise
main.dart=this is main.dart

request=https://api.github.com/repos/owner/repo/contents/alt_branch/dartpad_metadata.yaml?ref=some_branch
status=200
description=A Dart Exercise
main.dart=this is main.dart

request=https://api.github.com/repos/owner/repo/contents/alternate_path/dartpad_metadata.yaml
status=200
description=A Dart Exercise
test.dart=this is test.dart

request=https://api.github.com/repos/owner/repo/contents/unnecessary_file/dartpad_metadata.yaml
status=200
description=A Dart Exercise
unnecessary.txt=this is unnecessary.txt

feature7_4_repo_errors.json.description: Surfaces normalized error categories when an exercise cannot be loaded: a descriptor that is not valid structured data, a descriptor missing its file list, mode, or name, a descriptor that points at a file which does not exist, a descriptor path that itself does not exist, and an over-quota response. The output reports the first requested URL, the HTTP status, and the normalized error category.

feature8_1_dirty_tracking.json.description: An editable overlay on a code document that tracks whether it diverges from its backing document. It starts clean, becomes dirty after a field is changed, becomes clean again if the field is restored to its original value, and becomes clean when a new backing document is adopted. The output reports the dirty flag after each step.

feature8_2_create_gist.json.description: Produces a new immutable code document that captures the current edited state of an editable overlay. The output reports the field value carried into the newly created document.

feature8_3_clone.json.description: Creates an independent deep copy of a code document, preserving its identifier and description. The output reports the cloned document's identifier and description.

_constraints.input_condition: line_for_offset action with source and offsets array
_constraints.output_rule: Emits 'offset=<o> line=<l>' row for each offset; offsets >= last_line_length resolve to last zero-based line index

_constraints.input_condition: offset_for_line action with source and lines array
_constraints.output_rule: Emits 'line=<l> offset=<o>' row; lines >= last_line_index resolve to offset of last line

_constraints.input_condition: parse_snippet action with begin/end markers of form '{$ begin <name> $}' and '{$ end <name> $}'
_constraints.output_rule: Emits 'file=<name>' then file body lines; body MUST end with a trailing newline in output

_constraints.input_condition: parse_snippet action with no begin/end markers
_constraints.output_rule: Emits single 'file=main.dart' then trimmed content (leading/trailing whitespace stripped)

_constraints.input_condition: parse_snippet action with unbalanced markers (e.g. new begin without prior end)
css_options action with token containing 'run-dartpad' (optionally prefixed)
css_options action with token containing 'run-dartpad' and colon-delimited key-values
_constraints.output_rule: Emits 'valid=true' followed by one '<key>=<value>' line for each option; keys sorted alphabetically; options include ga_id, height, mode, run, split, theme, width

_constraints.input_condition: css_options action with token NOT containing 'run-dartpad' (e.g. 'run-flutterpad')
extract_html_body action with empty string html
extract_html_body action with well-formed HTML containing <body>...</body>
_constraints.output_rule: Emits 'len=<n>' then trimmed body content; body content MUST end with trailing newline in output

_constraints.input_condition: extract_html_body action with HTML missing <body> tag (e.g. <html><head>...)
extract_html_body action with non-HTML string
_constraints.output_rule: Emits 'len=<n>' where n is length of input string; input is returned unchanged

_constraints.input_condition: extract_html_body action with unclosed tag inside body (e.g. </XXX>)
_constraints.output_rule: Unclosed malformed tag is preserved verbatim in output; len matches excerpt length

_constraints.input_condition: bind action with source_type='stream', target_type='property'
_constraints.output_rule: Emits 'value=<last_event>' and 'updates=<count>' equal to number of events

_constraints.input_condition: bind action with source_type='property', initial value, flush=true
bind action with source_type='property', target_type='function', flush=true
bind action with events but cancel_after set to N
dep_retrieve action with value registration
dep_zone action
load_gist action with valid ID
load_gist action returning 404
load_gist action with rate_limited=true
load_sample action with valid id
load_sample action with unknown id
load_repo action with valid path
_constraints.output_rule: Emits 'request=URL', 'status=200', 'description=<desc>', '<filename>=<content>' for file listed in metadata

_constraints.input_condition: load_repo action with ref parameter
load_repo action with file parameter (overrides list)
load_repo action returning 200 but invalid metadata
load_repo action returning 404 for path
load_repo action with rate_limited=true
gist_dirty action with change flag
gist_dirty action with restore=true
gist_dirty action with set_backing=true
gist_create action
_constraints.output_rule: Emits 'description=<new_val>' where new_val is the value passed (bar)

_constraints.input_condition: gist_clone action with id and description
all output operations
_constraints.output_rule: Output MUST be line-oriented text; each line is 'key=value' or 'key=value value'; trailing newline mandatory on last line; NO PASS/FAIL or diagnostic words

hide.replacement: a list of forbidden keys to prevent overriding system state — review security policy for the complete exclusion set
